channel.title: Kinedu Blog — Real Guidance for Real Parents
channel.description: Baby development articles by pediatricians and child development experts.

item.title: What Is Tongue-Tie in a Baby?
item.description: Tongue-tie, or ankyloglossia, is a condition that can significantly impact a baby’s ability to feed, affecting both breastfeeding and bottle-feeding.

item.title: What are baby growth spurts?
item.description: As a mom, you will experience those moments when your baby seems to be fussy, hungry all the time, or extra clingy. These are signs of a growth spurt!

item.title: Why does the 6-Month Sleep Regression Happen?￼
item.description: Key Points: More frequent night wakings, difficulty falling back asleep and difficulty falling asleep at bed time and nap time Motor Development: roll

item.title: Why does the 4-Month Sleep Regression Happen?
item.description: Key Points: Sleep cycles have developed and are now longer: 90 minutes. They also go through 4 sleep stages instead of just 2. Cognitive development:

item.title: What Language Milestones is my 13-15 month-old developing?
item.description: At 13 months your baby is now officially a toddler, but of course they’re still your baby! After turning one, you’ll notice their language development
